76385.subject: 🟢 Robinhood chain flips Base as memecoins surge
76385.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $65,608 (-0.3%) | **ETH** $1,925 (0.2%) | **SOL** $78 (0.2%)

* Robinhood Chain [flips Base](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2079933174797242568) in daily active users

* ==SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce says some crypto vaults and onchain lending strategies may fall under ==[securities laws](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079941089117745375)

* Trump== threatens attacks on Iranian ====[power plants](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2079915469859889416)==== and bridges==

* ==Anthropic and AMD announce ====[$5B chip deal](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2079911256065008091?s=20)==

* ==The Senate has 15 days to pass the ==[Crypto Clarity Act](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2079934187306377413)== before summer recess — Latest draft includes provisions that would ====[ban presidents](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2079962982793052353?s=20)==== and federal official for issuing or sponsoring crypto products==

* ==Franklin Templeton calls AI ====[“crypto’s killer use case”](https://decrypt.co/374055/franklin-templeton-agentic-ai-crypto-killer-use-case)==

* ==Bitcoin ETFs extend ====[inflow streak](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2080143211117326833?s=20)==== to 7 days==

* ==House passes bill to ban Congress from ====[insider trading](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2080036698801488284?s=20)==

* ==BitMex announces September 23 ====[shutdown](https://decrypt.co/374125/bitmex-to-close-on-september-23-halts-new-sign-ups)==

* The US collects over [$13 billion](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2079938037274775826) from Venezuelan oil sales

———————————————————————————


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.dailybones.com/p/robinhood-chain-flips-base-as-memecoins-surge

Fear and Greed has ticked up to
39
(Fear)
and BTC is at
$65.5K
this morning. Bitcoin pulled back slightly overnight after
failing to hold above $66K, retracing as traders position
cautiously ahead of next week's Fed decision.

Regulatory pressure intensified yesterday on multiple fronts.
The CLARITY Act draft dropped at 616 pages. The SEC flagged DeFi
vaults as potential securities. FATF declared most DeFi platforms
are not truly decentralised. And Franklin Templeton made a bold
case for why AI agents need blockchain more than any other
infrastructure.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

The SEC Just Warned DeFi Vault Builders About Securities Law

SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce published a statement on July
22 warning that on-chain vaults and lending protocols may already
qualify as securities under federal law. She said putting a
financial product on a blockchain does not change its legal
classification.

Morpho fell roughly 5% on the day. Over $8.6 billion sits across
788 curated vaults serving 1.4 million users.

What test does Peirce say determines whether a vault qualifies
as a security?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/_qIVz8Weqmx9m48BrZZpzA~~/AAQRxRA~/iqgaTYrR36-trmcVoR-HewoJpE6aR9JAaDSnV-U9Nn5Da0wR-FyyTnaXKIW2VTBShJwmlKThijLfTNMS01jil0KS_FlwgH9aoxzL4sOXin4W-igiSOd3wZLuboM9s1M8Hyc-0hMaMIvYQPMtnrICE7_rW6xpL0aiISIqOn9BiWzcQs311HRn5yyAfnRjwLpqREv5Zwd4DU

76381.subject: The Ultimate Guide to Kimi K3 🤖
76381.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/kimi-k3-ultimate-guide

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 397k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
Last month, GLM-5.2 [ https://substack.com/redirect/c2213750-873b-4f21-a970-6f1440746498?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] delivered the ChatGPT moment for local AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/c2213750-873b-4f21-a970-6f1440746498?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]: the first open-weight model good enough to be a daily driver on your own hardware. Yet, it left one question hanging: what happens when an open lab stops merely catching up to the closed frontier and starts beating it? 🤔
Kimi K3 is the answer.
Released on July 16 by Moonshot AI, the Beijing lab behind the Kimi assistant, K3 is a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight flagship: the largest ever released, with a 1,048,576-token context window that swallows entire codebases and data rooms in one pass, native image and video understanding, and always-on reasoning.
Kimi K3 vs. Claude Fable 5. Credit: @shiri_shh via X
In its first week, independent testing produced results no open model has ever posted:
#1 in the world for frontend coding: 1,679 Elo on Arena, ahead of Claude Fable 5 (1,631) and GPT-5.6 Sol (1,618) in blind human voting (Arena)
#1 on confirmed task success across 8,000+ live agentic sessions (Arena Agent)
#1 in 3D design, 82 Elo ahead of second-place Fable 5 (Design Arena)
#2 in the world on agentic knowledge work, the spreadsheets-memos-presentations grind that fills every analyst’s week, ahead of GPT-5.6 Sol, Sonnet 5, and Opus 4.8 (Artificial Analysis)
Tied GPT-5.6 for the highest score ever recorded on CVE detection, at a fraction of the cost (Aikido)
#3 on the Intelligence Index, the highest placement an open-weight model has ever managed (Artificial Analysis)
All of that at roughly one-third of the price. Together AI’s engineering teardown found Fable 5-level software-engineering performance at ~35% of the cost, and Moonshot says the weights go up for free download on July 27.
Credit: Command Code via X
One caveat before the hype takes over. Like GLM-5.2’s maker ZAI, Moonshot is a Beijing-based lab, and the same sovereignty considerations flagged in the GLM-5.2 deep dive [ https://substack.com/redirect/c2213750-873b-4f21-a970-6f1440746498?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] apply here: open weights mitigate some of that risk; the hosted API does not.
But the model is only half the story, because K3 is also the least forgiving frontier model on the market: a long-horizon work engine, not a chatbot. Deliberately slow, reasoning at maximum effort by default, and dramatically better (and 10x cheaper, via cache discounts) the longer and more context
